scope: Conditional finite-population digital assembly acceptance under a center-distance-induced transverse backlash increment; not manufacturing yield.

population.estimand: Mean modeled acceptance probability over every feasible layout in the frozen test partition. No inference beyond this finite authored population is claimed.

inference.inference_unit: independent Sobol scramble of the complete held-out finite-layout census
inference.multiplicity: Bonferroni allocation across the two primary hypotheses

primary_interaction.contrast: [p(high shaft, high backlash)-p(high shaft, low backlash)]-[p(low shaft, high backlash)-p(low shaft, low backlash)] at zero housing erosion
primary_interaction.decision_rule: Supported only when the multiplicity-adjusted upper confidence limit is below -0.01.

primary_housing_equivalence.contrast: Maximum absolute paired acceptance difference between 0.0 and 0.1 mm erosion across the four shaft/backlash cells
primary_housing_equivalence.decision_rule: Supported only when simultaneous confidence intervals for all four paired differences lie within [-0.001, 0.001].

rationale: The v2 evidence is superseded because scenario identifiers collided and its training-prefix bootstrap lacked a population estimand. V3 uses the complete feasible test partition, collision-free cells, repeated randomized QMC, paired contrasts, explicit effect/equivalence margins, and familywise decision rules.
